briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for June 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🌱 Spreading Climate Action Messages and the Role of Corporations
sections.content: In today's key news, a prominent trend was observed where corporations actively spread messages on climate change response. LG Electronics took the lead in global environmental protection activities by screening videos urging participation in climate change response on outdoor billboards in major city centers such as Times Square in New York, USA, and Piccadilly in London, UK [1, 3, 5, 7, 13, 15]. This campaign, held in commemoration of World Environment Day, is evaluated as a positive example that utilizes 'LG Hope Screens' to raise awareness about the importance of the climate crisis, enhance ESG competitiveness, and prominently feature climate action messages [10, 17, 18].

sections.title: 🌱 Community-Led Strengthening of Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Various local governments and communities across the nation are continuing their active efforts to implement carbon neutrality. Suwon City declared an eco-friendly lifestyle practice movement [2, 4], and Gimpo City is accelerating its transition to carbon neutrality, building the foundation for a future eco-city [29, 44, 46]. In cities like Sejong and Incheon, activities were carried out to broaden consensus on carbon neutrality practices and spread a citizen culture [28, 32, 41], while Cheonan and Cheongju encouraged citizen participation through events like 'Carbon Zero Day' and 'No Disposable Items Day' [31, 42, 45, 49]. Jeju, for instance, emphasized the practice of carbon-neutral climate action through its Environment Day commemorative event [35], demonstrating diverse efforts at the local level to promote environmental conservation and a culture of carbon neutrality [38, 39, 40, 48].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Infrastructure Expansion
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating through domestic corporate efforts and infrastructure development. NH Nonghyup Bank is accelerating greenhouse gas reduction and renewable energy transition by securing 8MW of eco-friendly power, the largest in the banking sector, through a Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with Hanwha Solutions [47, 55, 57, 59, 81, 91, 94, 97]. Furthermore, Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) has completed a 52km transmission network connecting Muan and Sinan in Jeollanam-do, which is expected to alleviate renewable energy output curtailment issues in the Honam region and boost its supply [56, 58, 61, 72, 73, 75, 80, 82, 83, 88, 98]. With policy support, such as the Ministry of Climate acknowledging air-source heat as renewable energy and strengthening cooperation for heat pump dissemination [67, 70, 79], interest in resident-led renewable energy expansion is also high, with 129 villages applying for the 'Sunshine Income Village' project [90, 93, 104].

sections.title: ⚠️ Realistic Threats of Climate Change and Policy Challenges
sections.content: Realistic threats posed by climate change, along with policy discussions and challenges, have been reported. Domestically, the impact of the climate crisis on the Yeongcheon region was reported, and the risk of massive carbon emissions if mangrove forests are submerged due to sea-level rise was warned [22, 25]. Internationally, concerns were raised that flood seasons are shifting due to climate change, potentially leaving millions vulnerable [105]. Additionally, expert warnings indicated that climbing in the Himalayas is becoming more dangerous [112]. Meanwhile, Nuclear Europe argued that achieving climate goals is impossible with only renewable energy and without nuclear power [62], and news that former President Trump cut ocean monitoring programs essential for tracking climate change is raising concerns about international policy setbacks [110].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Response Efforts and Challenges
sections.content: The international community is facing various challenges alongside its cooperation efforts to address climate change. Island nations held an ocean summit, demonstrating efforts to cooperate and strengthen ties in the face of climate change [113]. Domestically, participants are being recruited for the Arctic Academy, which teaches about Arctic policy and climate change, thereby raising awareness of international climate issues [8]. However, in Europe, the construction of 'Northwind Mega,' a multi-billion euro offshore wind project, was halted due to allegations of EIA manipulation, causing setbacks in renewable energy expansion [102], and some reform parliaments have shown opposition to net-zero policies [107].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing covered positive movements by corporations and local communities in response to climate change, news of accelerated renewable energy transition, and the realistic threats of climate change and challenges for the international community. Emphasizing once again the importance of continuous attention and effort to overcome the climate crisis, this concludes today's climate briefing.
